articles.title: About
articles.contents:
I’m lucky to wear two hats; one as a Ph.D. candidate in Sociology at Stanford and the other of a research science intern at Intuit. In both of these roles I get the chance to do what I love, which is working with big, messy data and produce insightful analyses and research. My academic research and professional experiences in the tech and data space have allowed me to work with different types of data, including satellite imagery, U.S. Census data, state voter records, social media data, and workplace communication data (e.g., Zoom, Slack, Outlook). Working with different types of data and telling stories with data are the common themes running through all of my experiences.
As a Ph.D. candidate, much of my time is focused on running analyses and conducting research for my dissertation. My dissertation is concerned with what makes vibrant and attractive public spaces that draw people from a variety of backgrounds and neighborhoods. I draw on satellite imagery, foot-traffic data, and U.S. Census data and apply geospatial data science and statistical methods in my research. You can see some of my work in my portfolio.
In the last two years I have sought out opportunities outside of academia to grow my data analytics and storytelling skills. I have served as a GIS/Spatial Data Analyst for the Texas Democrats and a research science intern for Intuit People Analytics Team in order to prepare for the next phase of my career. My resume and portfolio provide more details about the skills and abilities that I have developed in these positions.
Personal Background
My fascination with human movement patterns and what makes vibrant public spaces stems from my own experience living in different parts of the country and comparing the physical landscapes and public spaces across different regions of the country. My geographic biography is highlighted in the map below. I grew up in a region of south-central Texas called the Texas Hill Country, which lies between San Antonio and Austin. My first major move was to Philadelphia to complete my undergraduate degree in Sociology at the University of Pennsylvania. After completing my bachelor’s degree, I swapped coasts to move to Palo Alto for the opportunity pursue a Ph.D. at Stanford University. A few months after the Covid-19 pandemic began, I moved back to south-central Texas for one year to help my parents while continuing my studies. I recently moved back to the San Francisco Peninsula to finish out my Ph.D. and begin my career as a geospatial scientist.
[INSERT MAP ABOUT HERE]
Outside of work, my favorite things include: spending time with my family, exercising, hiking, cooking vegan food, reading, and avidly following the NBA.

            Analytics | Research | Geospatial Science
            I am a geospatial scientist and researcher analyzing big data to discover new insights about the demographics of human movement in and around our shared public spaces. Currently I am working on my doctoral dissertation at Stanford and interning as a research scientist at Intuit. I have created this website to act as a container and digital display case for my projects, educational and professional experiences, and digital garden. I hope that you find something that sparks your interest while navigating around my website.
